what_it_does: Records the analysis of a third pitch-deck ingest folder ('recent-pitches', 49 pages, 3:2 aspect ratio, high level-of-detail) used as a stress test of the design-language model, reporting whether density/detail is coupled to aspect ratio and whether the LOD ladder holds a third rung.
observations: States the central verdict: the design language is ratio-invariant and the LOD ladder gains a third high-detail rung.
Reports measured values: side margins ~12% (11.6%), zoning wash #faf9f7, base #fdfdfd, gold ramp #c4ab43/#c49649, all unchanged at 3:2 (1893x1287).
Concludes ratio and density/LOD are correlated by choice, not mechanically coupled.
Headlines a finding that slides ARE templates: p-25 and p-49 share identical 'Our Entry Markets' layout filled with different data.

Lists new atoms/patterns: QR 'Virtual Tour' card, two bullet semantics (gold triangle vs gold arrow flow-bullet), quadrant/positioning map, team/org grid, geo locator, multi-section slide, device-channel showcase row.
Walks the 0-9 layer deltas (Frame/Grid/Zoning/Colour/Atoms/Patterns/Sequence) confirming each against prior folders.
Marks the AXES.md open question 'density-perp-ratio?' as resolved: independent.
standouts: Slide-as-template proof via two identically-laid-out slides with swapped data (p-25/p-49).
Bullet glyph carries semantics: triangle = plain list, arrow = causal/flow list.
Explicit resolution of an open generative question (density independent of ratio).
conventions: Uses a 0-9 numbered-layer diff format shared with sibling analysis notes (pitch-deck.md).
Uses checkmark/plus/pencil/warning glyphs to classify findings (confirmed / new / generalises / contradiction).
Cross-references sibling docs (SYSTEM-GAPS.md, AXES.md) rather than restating.
Frames findings as answers to pre-set stress-test questions.
notes: An evidence log proving the design language holds at a new aspect ratio and a higher detail level, and that decks are assembled from reusable, data-swappable slide templates.
questions: What are the 13 archetypes / baseline atoms referenced as already established elsewhere?
Where is the ingest sample data physically (ingest/recent-pitches/) and is it still present?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the note is framed as a stress test and delivers stress-test results with measured evidence.
apparent_themes: Generative model where design = f(content, axis)
intention_signals: Trying to confirm, with a deliberately different sample, that the extracted design DNA generalises across aspect ratios and detail levels, and to harvest new reusable atoms/archetypes before synthesising the system.
novelty: The empirical template-equivalence proof (two slides, same layout, different data) and the semantic-bullet-glyph distinction.
contribution: Contributes the ratio-invariance finding, the LOD-ladder third rung, the slide-as-template evidence, and a set of new design atoms (QR card, flow-bullet, quadrant map, team grid, geo locator, channel strip) to the fused design-language model.
summary_for_embedding: Analysis note recording that a 3:2 high-detail pitch-deck folder confirms the design language is ratio- and density-invariant, proves slides are reusable data-swappable templates, and adds new design atoms.
intention_for_embedding: To stress-test and validate the extracted design DNA against a differently-shaped, denser sample before synthesising it into one generative system.
